prompt: A video display that is limited to black and white only with no shades of gray is called ____.
What is the approximate number of frames that must be presented each second for the human eye to perceive the display without flickering?
What was the original purpose for ultrasound system's analog scan converter?
choices: to increase the dynamic range of ultrasound systems
to increase the sensitivity of ultrasound systems
to allow for gray scale imaging

prompt: Which of the following is best described as an analog number?
choices: the number of people in a room
the number of stars in the sky
the number of tires on a car

prompt: True or False? A digital representation of a number can achieve only specific fixed values.
True or False? Digital scan converters do not use computer technology to process electronic data into images.

Two digital scan converters are undergoing evaluation. Both produce images of the same size. System A has 1,000,000 pixels with 10 bits assigned to each. System B has 250,000 pixels with 12 bits assigned to each. Which system is more likely to have the capability to display very small details in an image?

Two digital scan converters are undergoing evaluation. System A has 1,000,000 pixels, with 4 bits assigned to each. System B has 250,000 pixels with 12 bits assigned to each. Which system is more likely to display images with very subtle differences in gray scale?
A byte is composed of _____ bits. A word is composed of _____ bytes.
True or False? The greater the number of bits assigned to each pixel of a digital image, the greater the spatial resolution of the image is.
What happens to an image when the number of bits allocated to each pixel increases?
What is the maximum number of shades of gray that are represented with 4 bits?
With 6 bits, what is the largest number of different shades of gray that can be stored?
How many bits are required to display 10 different shades of gray? 14 shades? 15 shades?
What is the number of binary digits required to store 29 levels of gray?
True or False? For digital image information to be displayed on a conventional television screen, the data must be processed by a digital-to-analog converter.
True or False? Preprocessing of image data occurs after the data has been stored in the scan converter.
True or False? Post-processing of image data occurs after data has been stored in the scan converter.
All of the following are true of read zoom except:
choices: same number of pixels are in the original ROI and the zoomed image
on the screen, the pixels in the zoomed image are larger than those in the ROI
the same number of ultrasound pulses are used to create the original ROI and the zoomed image

prompt: All of the following are true of write zoom except:
choices: same number of pixels are in the original ROI and the zoomed image
the pixel size in the zoomed image and the ROI are the same
in comparison to the zoomed image, fewer ultrasound pulses are used to create the original ROI

prompt: True or False? The assignment of different shades of gray to the digital numbers stored in the scan converter acts to improve the diagnostic value of an ultrasound exam.
The relationship between the largest and the smallest signal amplitudes processed by a component of an ultrasound system is called the _____ and has units of ____.
Which of these components typically has the greatest dynamic range?
Which of the following components typically has the lowest dynamic range?
Why is it necessary for the receiver to perform compression on the electrical signals that it processes?
choices: the dynamic range of the system's electronics is less than the dynamic range of the received sound reflection
the display's dynamic range exceeds the transducer's dynamic range
the dynamic range of the receiver is less than the dynamic range of the image processor
the dynamic range of the display exceeds the dynamic range of all other components of the ultrasound system

prompt: A signal with a 120 dB dynamic range is compressed by 40 dB. What is the dynamic range of the compressed signal?
A signal with a 120 dB dynamic range was previously compressed by 40 dB. What is the dynamic range of the original, uncompressed signal?
Which display is limited to a single pair of brightness or display levels?
All of the following are consistent with an image containing black and white only with no shades of gray except:
Which of the following terms is not associated with a wide dynamic range image?
